Generic In Person Cold Call Script for Cert III
Compliance Requirements
1) 	You may not use the word, FREE, to describe this program
2) 	You must always use a hedge-word (i.e. almost, basically, virtually) if you
refer to “100% state funding.” (It’s actually 99.33% funding)
3) 	You must disclose the $20 student fee ($10 for concessionals), which can be
paid up front or upon completion of the program.
Compliance-Approved Language-Pattern
Hey, my name is ___________, from _________ (your companies name)? How you
going today, mate?
Okay, great! Now, mate, we work in conjunction with the state government and, right
now, I’ve got a certificate 3 for ____________ available and it’s being funded by the
state. Have you heard about the state funding at all?
Oh—okay! Well, mate, you’re eligible, right now, for a Certificate 3 in ___________ …
and virtually the entire cost is being funded by the state. Now, normally, it’s a $_,000
course, but with the government behind it right now, the timing is perfect. Are you
interested?
Okay, great, mate! (Take out an application and begin writing in his answers to your
questions.)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

So, let me just ask you a couple of quick questions, to make sure you qualify for the state
funding.
Now, first, you are a resident of (their location), correct?
Okay, excellent. (Check it off the application sheet). And your full name is…?
Okay—excellent. (Your full name) Nice to meet you! (Smile and shake his hand.) And,
mate, do you have any other qualifications … either a Cert 3 or Cert 4?
And did you get that before or after high school?
Okay, excellent. And how many years have you been _________, mate … just a
ballpark?
Okay, excellent! Well, mate, based on everything you just said to me, you definitely
qualify for the state funding.
Now, getting started is very simple. It’s just a question of some basic information to get
the application going, and then we can handle everything else for you after that. So let me
just tell you just a little bit more about the actual training, because the program itself is
basically second to none. It’ll take me less than sixty seconds. Sound good?
Okay, excellent. Now, first of all, our trainers will do everything at your convenience—
starting with them coming to you, to make sure they minimize any downtime. And they’ll
come day or night, on weekdays or the weekend; and you can also choose the place—
your office, your home, or any job site you’re working on; whatever suits you best. You
follow me sense so far?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 4 --

Okay, great. Now, in terms of the actual training, they’re obviously not there to teach you
how to ________. Considering how long you’ve been _________, they’re not gonna
waste your time with the basics. I mean, after ___ years on the job, you probably know
80% to 90% of what’s on the qualification, so what the trainers will do is sit down with
you and go through everything, and sign you off on the things you already know. called
It’s “Recognition of Prior Learning, and it totally streamlines the process, so you don’t
waste any time. Makes sense?
Okay, excellent! And then, for the things that you do need to learn—meaning, the other
10% to 20%—the trainer will use “gap training,” to fill in any holes. And, bam, just like
that, you’ll have your nationally recognized qualification for _________, which, by the
way, you totally deserve to have. I mean, you’re paying for this out of your tax dollars
anyway, so it’s important to us that you get every bit of value out it.
And that’s it, mate: If you’re even half as thrilled as the rest of the ________who’ve been
through this program, then you’re going to be very, very impressed. Sound fair enough?
Great! So I just need some basic information, to do the application, and then we can
handle everything else for you after that. Now, first, do you have your driver’s license
handy?
As you complete the application, you want to be doing four things:
1) 	Building deeper and deeper rapport
2) 	Reinforcing the value and benefits of the program
3) 	Reinforcing the urgency of doing it now.
4) 	Finish by explaining the student fee and giving them the option to
pay now or after the training.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 4 --

How to Ask for the Student Fee
Okay, great! Now, mate, like I said before, the state pays for basically the entire training,
including the cost of issuing the certificate. The tuition itself is just under $_,000, which
the state pays us for directly, so there’s absolutely no out of pocket cost to you there
whatsoever. The only thing the state does require is that you pay a nominal student fee,
which is only $20, believe it or not. I know it seem kind of irrelevant, but the state still
wants you to pay this, so have at least a tiny bit of skin in the game. (Shake your head, as
if it defies logic.)
Anyway, you don’t have to pay it now, if you don’t want to; you can pay it as you go
through the training, if you’d like—or you can pay it at the end, whatever’s easiest for
you.
Anyway, it’s only $20, so it’s no big deal. If you’d like to pay it now, I can give you a
receipt, or we can just as easily take care of it during the training. Either way, you’ll be
getting a national qualification, for about the cost of a six-pack! Sound good?
Okay, great! So do you want take care of it now, or after you’ve been through some
training?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 4 --
